title: The One With The Thanksgiving Flashbacks

lines.text: Monica and Rachel's, everyone has just finished Thanksgiving dinner and are groaning over their fullness.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Monica that was the best Thanksgiving dinner ever! I think you killed us.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I couldn't possibly eat another bite.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I need something sweet.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Does anyone wanna watch TV?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Yeah, sure.
She starts pushing the power button on the remote, but it's not facing the TV so it doesn't work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Monica your remote doesn't work.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, you have to lift it and point.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh. Aw, forget it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, you know what we should all do? We should play that game where everyone says one thing that they're thankful for.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ooh-ooh, I! I am thankful for this beautiful fall we've been having.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's very nice.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's sweet, Joey.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, the other day I was at the bus-stop and this lovely fall breeze came in out of nowhere and blew this chick's skirt right up. Oh! Which reminds me, I'm also thankful for thongs. (Note: Actually, I think every guy is thankful for thongs. That and spandex. J )
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, continued from earlier. Joey is talking about the wonder that is the thong.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I mean, it's not so much an underpant as it is a feat of engineering. I mean, it's amazing how much they can do with so little material! And the way they play with your mind! Is it there? Is it not there?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you aware that you're still talking?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is anyone thankful for anything else besides a thong?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh, I don't know what to pick. Am I more thankful for my divorce or my eviction? Hmm.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wow! See, and I didn't think you'd be able to come up with anything.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm sorry. It's just that this is the worse Thanksgiving ever.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no! I am the king of bad Thanksgivings. You can't just swoop in here with your bad marriage and take that away from me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, you're not gonna tell the whole story about how your parents got divorced again are you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh God, no.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, come on! I wanna hear it! It wouldn't be Thanksgiving without Chandler bumming us out!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's a tradition, like the parade. If the parade decided it was gay, moved out, and abandoned its entire family.
And with that, we start a series of flashbacks to Thanksgiving's of years gone by.
Thanksgiving 1978
The Bing household, Mr. and Mrs. Bing and Young Chandler are eating Thanksgiving dinner as a housekeeper serves them.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Bing
lines.text: Now Chandler dear, just because your father and I are getting a divorce it doesn't mean we don't love you. It just means he would rather sleep with the house-boy than me.

lines.speaker: The Housekeeper
lines.text: More turkey Mr. Chandler? (And he makes eyes at him.)
Present Day
Monica and Rachel's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're right. Yours is worse. You are the king of bad Thanksgivings.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I don't know about that. I've got one that's worse.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really? Worse than, "More turkey Mr. Chandler?"

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, did the little rich boy have a problem with the butler? Yes, mine's worse!
Thanksgiving 1862
A Union battlefield hospital, Phoebe, in a past life, is tending to a wounded Union soldier. (By the way, for historical perspective, 1862 was the second year of the American Civil War.)

lines.speaker: Past Life Phoebe
lines.text: More bandages! More bandages! Please, can I get some more bandages in here! This man is dying-(She is cut off by an exploding shell just outside the tent. When the smoke clears, she's missing an arm and the blood is pumping out like you'd see in a horror movie. And upon seeing her condition, she says...) Oh no.
Present Day
Monica and Rachel's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: In this life, Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, this life! Oh okay no, Chandler's is worse.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Man, it must be so cool remembering stuff like that! I don't have any past life memories.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Of course you don't sweetie. You're brand new.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know Monica's worst Thanksgiving.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, let's not tell this story.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Oh, come on!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, I know! I know! It's the one where Joey got Monica's turkey stuck on his head!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?! Joey got a turkey stuck on his head?!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, it's not like it sounds.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's exactly like it sounds.
Thanksgiving 1992
Monica and Phoebe's, Phoebe is entering.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (muffled) Hello?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (surprised) Hello?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Phoebe?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Joey? What's going on?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look. (He walks out of the bathroom with his head stuck in a huge turkey.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I know! It's stuck!!!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (walks him to the kitchen) Easy. Step. How did it get on?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I put it on to scare Chandler!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! Monica's gonna totally freak out!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well then help me get it off! Plus, it smells really bad in here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, of course it smells really bad. You have your head up a dead animal.
They hear Monica trying to unlock the door. So Phoebe quickly pushes his head down onto the table to make it look like the turkey is just sitting on a platter and not stuck on Joey's head.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey, did you get the turkey basted-Oh my God! Oh my God! (She sees someone is stuck in the turkey.) Who is that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: It's Joey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What-what are you doing? Is this supposed to be funny?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, it's not supposed to be funny, it's supposed to be scary.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, get it off now!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can't! It-it's stuck!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I don't care! That-that turkey has to feed 20 people at my parent's house and they're not gonna eat it off your head!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, hold on! Okay, let's just all think.

They all start thinking. Joey starts rubbing his chin, of course his chin is currently inside the turkey so he ends up rubbing the turkey. And I didn't do that joke one bit of justice. It's one of those you have to see it to get it jokes.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I got it. Phoebe? All right, you pull. I'm gonna spread the legs as wide as I can. (Joey starts giggling.) Joey? Now is not the time!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sorry! Sorry.
They get into position to pull the turkey off.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, count to three. 1. 2. 3!
They both pull but Joey slips out and starts to fall backwards just as Chandler enters, scaring him.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Arghhhhhh!! (Joey turns around to taunt him, but Chandler is in the doorway and Joey is facing the kitchen.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (pointing) It worked! I scared ya, I knew it! Ha-ha!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm over here big guy.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (turning all the way around, and still not facing Chandler) Yeah, you are! (Starts dancing.) I scared you!
Present Day
Monica and Rachel's, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (laughing) You did look like an idiot.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, I wasn't the only one who looked like an idiot. All right? Remember when Ross tried to say, "Butternut squash?" And it came out, "Squatternut buash?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah that's the same.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's it. That's my worse Thanksgiving.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh wait! That can't be the one Rachel's talking about. She didn't even know that happened. So which one was it?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Which one?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, I-I really don't want to tell this story.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, come on Monica, reliving past pain and getting depressed is what Thanksgiving is all about. Y'know, for me anyway. And of course, the Indians.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look umm, of all people, you do not want me to tell this story!
Thanksgiving 1987
The Geller household, Mr. and Mrs. Geller are getting ready for Thanksgiving dinner. The doorbell rings.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Monica! I think Rachel's here!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'll get it! (She runs in, and she's her old fat self like The One With The Prom Video. Not only that, she's out of breath after running a short distance. She goes over and opens the door to reveal Rachel with her old nose.) Happy Thanksgiving!

lines.speaker: Big Nosed Rachel
lines.text: Not for me. Chip and I broke up!

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: Oh, why? Why? What happened?

lines.speaker: Big Nosed Rachel
lines.text: Well, you know that my parents are out of town and Chip was going to come over...

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, and you were going him y'know, your flower.

lines.speaker: Big Nosed Rachel
lines.text: Okay, Monica, can you just call it sex?! It really creeps me out when you call it that! Okay, and by the way, while we're at it, a guy's thing is not called his tenderness. Believe me! (Walks into the living room and greets Monica's parents.) Hi!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Hi Rachel!

lines.speaker: Big Nosed Rachel
lines.text: Happy Thanksgiving!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: You too sweethart!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey!
He brought home Chandler for Thanksgiving. Chandler is sporting the very popular Flock of Seagulls haircut. Yeah, it's another you have to see it to believe it kinda thing.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Oh my!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, everyone, this is Chandler! My roommate and lead singer of our band!

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: Ross! (Wanting to be introduced.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, this is Monica.

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: Hi, I'm Ross's little sister.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (seeing her) Okay.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: I'm so glad you could come Chandler, we've got plenty of food so I hope you're hungry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, mom. Mom. Chandler hates Thanksgiving and doesn't eat any Thanksgiving food.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh, well, I'm so glad you brought him here then.

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: Umm, Chandler, if you want I can make you some macaroni and cheese for dinner.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, as long as the pilgrims didn't eat it, I'm in.
As she is drinking, Monica laughs and Chandler's joke and Diet Coke comes out of her nose.

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: dammit! (Runs off.)
Ross points out Rachel to Chandler and goes over to talk to her. Rachel is checking out her nose in her compact mirror.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So uh, Rach? Does it, does it feel weird around here now? Y'know since I've been away at college.

lines.speaker: Big Nosed Rachel
lines.text: Oh! No, not really.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Well, that's cool. So did... (She walks away from him and he shuts up.)
Rachel wanders into the kitchen where Monica is making Chandler's dinner.

lines.speaker: Big Nosed Rachel
lines.text: Ugh! I cannot believe Chip dumped me for that slut Nancy Branson. I am never going out with him again. I don't care how much he begs!

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: I think his begging days are over now that he's going out with Nancy Branson.

lines.speaker: Big Nosed Rachel
lines.text: Y'know what? I've just had it with high school boys! They are just silly. (Ross is overhearing this.) Silly, stupid boys! I'm going to start dating men!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm, I'm sorry Judy, I couldn't find that bowl that you and Jack were looking for.

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: Call them mom and dad you loser!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (in a high pitched voice) Monica!
[Time lapse, dinner has finished and Chandler is sitting on the couch eating some pie. Monica sits down beside him, and he gets pushed up a little by the wave she makes in the couch.]

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: Hey Chandler! Did you like the macaroni and cheese?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh yeah, it was great. You should be a chef.

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: Okay!
He gets up and walks away as Rachel come running over all excited.

lines.speaker: Big Nosed Rachel
lines.text: Guess what?! All that stuff about Nancy Branson being a slut was all a rumor so Chip dumped her and he wants to come over to my house tonight!

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: Oh that's so great!

lines.speaker: Big Nosed Rachel
lines.text: I know!

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: Oh gosh, listen if you and Chip do it tonight, promise me you'll tell me everything.

lines.speaker: Big Nosed Rachel
lines.text: Oh totally, totally. Y'know it's not that big of deal, we already kinda did it once y'know.

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: I know, but y'know, this time you're gonna definitely know whether or not you did it!

lines.speaker: Big Nosed Rachel
lines.text: I know, I know. And oh, and this time Chip promised that-that this time it will last at least for an entire song!
[Cut to the kitchen, Ross and Chandler are doing the dishes.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So I'm thinking about asking Rachel out tonight. Y'know maybe play her that song we wrote last week.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Emotional Knapsack?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Right on! Oh! Uh, but, don't take to long okay? 'Cause uh, we're gonna test out our fake ID's tonight, right Clifford Alverez.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Listen, Roland Chang, if things go well, I'm gonna be out with her all night.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Dude, don't do that too me!
Monica enters behind them.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: All right, it's cool you can stay here. My parents won't mind.
Monica suddenly gets very happy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, it's not that, I just don't want to be stuck here all night with your fat sister.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey!
Upon hearing this, Monica starts to break down and storms out. Only to be stopped by her parents.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: (holding two pies) Monica, why don't you finish off these pies? I don't have any more room left in the fridge.

lines.speaker: Fat Monica
lines.text: No. No, thank you!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Well Judy, you did it! She's finally full!
Commercial Break
Monica and Rachel's, back to the present day.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I called you fat?! I don't even remember that!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, I do.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am so sorry. I really am. I was an idiot back then. I rushed the stage at a Wham concert for crying out loud!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I can't believe you called her fat.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I can't believe you let George Michael slap you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am really sorry. That is so terrible. I am so, so sorry.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Actually, y'know that's not the Thanksgiving I was talking about.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, it was!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, it wasn't. It was actually the...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (interrupting her) Okay, now Thanksgiving's over, let's get ready for Christmas. Who wants to go get a Christmas tree?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, no, I have the cutest Christmas story!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: We wanna hear Monica's Thanksgiving story!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fine, all right, mine had a dwarf that got broke in half, but y'know whatever.
Thanksgiving 1988
The Geller household kitchen, Mrs. Geller is cooking and Rachel, post nose job, is helping her.

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: So Rachel, your mom tells me you changed your major again.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, yeah, I had too. There was never any parking by the Psychology building.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (entering) Hi Rachel.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh hi!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Wow, love your new nose!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Jack.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: What? Dr. Wilson's an artist! He removed my mole cluster. Wanna see? (He starts to show her as the doorbell rings.)

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: I'll get it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, God! Please, let me! (Runs out.)

She opens the door to reveal Chandler and Ross. Unfortunately, they seem to have their holidays mixed up. They think it's Halloween and they're going as Crockett and Tubbs from that legendary TV show of the late 80's, Miami Vice. God, we looked silly back then!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey. (To his parents) Happy Thanksgiving!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: (To Chandler) God, your hair sure is different!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, we were just talking about that. I can't believe how stupid we used to look. (They both quickly push their sleeves over their elbows.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: So uh, where's Monica?

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: She's upstairs. Monica! Come down! Everyone's here! Ross, Rachel, and the boy who hates Thanksgiving.
Monica enters, but she forgot something. Oh, about 150 pounds. In other words, she lost weight, big time!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi, Chandler.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What-what's the matter? Is there, is there something on my dress? (She turns around making sure he gets a good look.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You just, you look so different! Terrific! That dress! That body!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Dude!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sorry!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Yes, yes Monica is thin. It's wonderful. But what we really want to hear about is Ross's new girlfriend.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh mom! Okay, umm, her name is Carol. And she's really pretty. And smart. And uh, she's-she's on the lacrosse team and the golf team. Can you believe it? She plays for both teams!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So Chandler, I guess I'll see you at dinner.
She heads for the kitchen and Chandler watches her leave and admires the view.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Dude!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sorry.
In the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (entering) Oh-ho, my God! That was so awesome! You totally got him back for calling you fat! He was just drooling all over you. That must've felt so great!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well it didn't!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I mean yeah, I look great. Yeah, I feel great and yeah, my heart is not in trouble anymore! Blah, blah, blah! Y'know I still don't feel like I got him back, y'know? I just want to humiliate him. I wanna, I want him to be like naked and then I'm going to point at him and laugh!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, that we may be able to do.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: How?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well guys tend to get naked before they're gonna have sex.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! I mean, I didn't work this hard and-and-and lose all this weight so that I can give my flower to someone like him!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, first of all, if you keep calling it that, no one's gonna ever take it. Then, second of all you're not actually gonna have sex with him! You're just gonna make him think that you are.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And when he's naked I can throw him out in the front yard and lock the door and all the neighbors will just humiliate him!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Then, you will definitely get him back!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, so how do I make him think I wanna have sex with him?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, oh, here's what you do. Just act like everything around you turns you on.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, like anything can be sexy. Like umm, oh-oh, like this dishtowel! (She grabs it and starts rubbing it on her cheek.) Ooh, ooh, this feels sooo good against my cheek! And-and if I feel a little hot, I can just dab myself with it. Or I can bring it down to my side and bring it through my fingers while I talk to him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (excited) I can do that!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah? Okay! Good, good, because he's coming. He's coming. (To Chandler) Hey, what's up? (She leaves and closes the door behind her.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica, I was wondering if you can make me some of that righteous mac and cheese like last year.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Umm, I'd love too! (She goes over and picks up the box and decides to follow Rachel's advice and holds the box up to her cheek.) Ooh, I love macaroni and cheese. I love-I love the way this box feels against my cheek.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Boy, I love carrots! Oh! (She picks up a bunch of them and holds them between her fingers.) Sometimes I like to put them between my fingers like this and-and hold them down here while I talk to you. (She is rubbing her hip with the carrots.) Umm, and-and-and y'know if I get really hot umm, I-I like to pick up this knife (She picks up a knife without putting the box down. She's holding the box between her cheek and shoulder) and-and umm, I-I put the cold steal against umm, (Pause) my body. (She doesn't have any exposed skin within reach of the knife, so while holding the carrots in one hand and the box between her face and shoulder, she rubs the knife on her stomach.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you all right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yeah, of course. I'm fine it's just that-(She drops the box and in a reflex action tries to catch it with her arm, the knife slips out and slowly flips through the air and comes point first down into Chandler's shoe.)
The hospital, Chandler has been rushed to the emergency room.

lines.speaker: The Doctor
lines.text: What do we got here?

lines.speaker: The Paramedic
lines.text: Twenty year old has got a severed toe on his right foot.
They go through the doors into the trauma room, opening them by ramming the gurney through them, only Chandler's foot is hanging off the end and he screams in pain.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Can you please not do that feet first? You know where his injury is! Severed toe, you just said it!

lines.speaker: The Doctor
lines.text: It says here that the knife went right through your shoe.

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Of course it did. They're made of wicker.

lines.speaker: The Doctor
lines.text: Did you bring the toe?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh yes! I have it right here, on ice! (She takes a bag of ice out of her purse and hands it to the doctor.)

lines.speaker: The Doctor
lines.text: (opening it) Don't worry son, we'll just attach it and-(Stops suddenly.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?! What is it?

lines.speaker: The Doctor
lines.text: You brought a carrot.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: The Doctor
lines.text: This isn't your toe, this is a small, very cold piece of carrot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You brought a carrot?!

lines.speaker: Mrs. Geller
lines.text: Oh my God! There's a toe in my kitchen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: God, I'm sorry! I'll go back and get it!

lines.speaker: The Doctor
lines.text: It's too late, all we can do now is sow up the wound.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Without my toe?! I need my toe!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait, no-no-no, I can go really fast! Dad, give me the keys to your Porsche!

lines.speaker: Mr. Geller
lines.text: Oh, I'm not falling for that one!
Present Day
Monica and Rachel's, Chandler is reacting to the story.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's why I lost my toe?! Because I called you fat?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I didn't mean to cut it off. It was an accident.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's why for an entire year people called me Sir Limps-A-Lot?!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry! It wasn't your whole toe!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well, I miss the tip! It's the best part. It has the nail. (He storms out.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler! (Follows him out.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Joey) Sir Limps-A-Lot, I came up with that.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You're a dork.
[Cut to the hallway, Chandler is standing in front of his door.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't believe this.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, I said I was sorry.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, well, sorry doesn't bring back the little piggy that cried all the way home! I hate this stupid day! And everything about it! I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh wait, Chandler, come here is there anything I can do? Anything?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, just leave me alone for a while. (He goes into his apartment.)
Chandler, Joey, and Ross's, Chandler is sitting on one of the chairs and the duck is running around him and quacking.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh-oh, I'm a duck! I go, "Quack, quack!" I'm happy all the time!
There's a knock on the door and Chandler gets up to answer it. He opens the door to reveal Monica with a turkey over her head.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nice try.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wait, wait, wait! (She puts a Shriner's hat on the turkey.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look, Monica...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Look! (She puts a big, yellow pair of sunglasses on the bird.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is not going to work.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I bet this will work! (She starts dancing and Chandler cracks up.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You are so great! I love you!
Monica stops suddenly and turns around slowly.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nothing! I said, I said "You're so great" and then I just, I just stopped talking!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You said you loved me! I can't believe this!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No I didn't!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes, you did!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No I didn't!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You love me!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No I don't! Stop it! Stop it! Stop it! Stop it!
Joey walks in and sees Monica. He freaks out and runs back into the hallway, screaming.
Ending Credits
Thanksgiving 1915
The Western front during World War I, Phoebe, in yet another past life, is once again a nurse tending to yet another dying soldier. But this time she's doing it with a French accent.

lines.speaker: French Phoebe
lines.text: Gauze! Gauze! I need to get some gauze in here! Can I please get some gauze in here! (A shell explodes outside next to the tent and when the smoke clears, Phoebe still has her arm.) Whew! (Her arm falls off and starts pumping out blood.) This is getting ridiculous uh!
End
